title: gitmap-v28 scan output
description: Stable schema for the JSON array produced by `gitmap-v28 scan` (default file: .gitmap/output/gitmap.json). The CSV sibling (.gitmap/output/gitmap.csv) carries the same fields in the column order documented in scan-output.README.md. Field names and JSON keys are stable contracts — automation may rely on them. New fields are additive and appended at the end of the CSV column list; existing field names are never renamed or repurposed.

id.description: Stable database row id assigned by the local SQLite store. Zero for records that have not yet been persisted (e.g. dry-run output).

slug.description: Lowercase, dash-separated, filesystem-safe identifier derived from repoName. Safe for use in file names and URLs.

repoId.description: Provider-side repository identifier (e.g. GitHub numeric id) when discoverable from the remote URL or API; empty string when unknown.

repoName.description: Human-readable repository name, typically the basename of the working tree.

httpsUrl.description: Canonical HTTPS clone URL. Empty string when no HTTPS remote could be derived.

sshUrl.description: Canonical SSH clone URL (git@host:owner/repo.git). Empty string when no SSH remote could be derived.

discoveredUrl.description: Raw remote URL exactly as it appeared in the repo's git config (origin remote). Preserved verbatim before normalization into httpsUrl/sshUrl.

branch.description: Branch the record will clone or operate on. Resolution order is recorded in branchSource.

branchSource.description: How `branch` was determined. 'head' = repo HEAD ref, 'config-default' = config.json defaultBranch, 'flag-default' = --default-branch CLI flag, 'fallback' = built-in default. Empty when unset (legacy rows).

relativePath.description: Path to the working tree relative to the scan root, using forward slashes.

absolutePath.description: Absolute on-disk path to the working tree at scan time. Platform-native separators.

cloneInstruction.description: Ready-to-run shell command that recreates this clone (mode-aware: HTTPS or SSH per --mode/config).

notes.description: Free-form annotation. Defaults to config.json `notes`; may be overridden per record by downstream tooling.

depth.description: Directory depth at which the repo was found, counted from the scan root (0 = scan root itself). A row whose depth equals the active --max-depth cap is a candidate for a deeper rescan.

transport.description: URL-scheme bucket of discoveredUrl. Mirrors the three-bucket collapse used by the clone-from terminal summary so JSON/CSV filters stay consistent with the TUI.
